title: dev_brief input
description: One development brief plus its run configuration. The brief bounds and specifies the work; the config names the repo, the mechanical gate battery, and the adversarial review lenses.

id.description: Stable brief id; the work lands on branch dev/<id>.

objective.description: What to build/fix and why — the developer's marching orders.

context.description: Background: root cause for bugs, prior art, constraints discovered elsewhere.

pointers.description: Where to look: files, modules, docs.

scope_out.description: What must NOT change (no-reorg boundaries, frozen surfaces).

acceptance.description: The criteria the adversarial reviewers hold the diff to.

repo_root.description: Absolute path of the git repository the brief runs against.

base_branch.description: Branch the brief worktree is based on.

gates.description: The mechanical gate battery, run in order in the workspace; pass = exit 0. Configure per repo (e.g. cargo fmt / cargo clippy -D warnings / cargo test).

max_fix_cycles.description: Developer-round budget: the initial implementation plus every loop-back.

lenses.description: Adversarial review lenses, each run CONCURRENTLY as its own child workflow. Omit (or leave empty) for the default trio: correctness, regressions, brief_compliance.

charter.description: The lens's marching orders: what failure class it hunts.
